mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling for managing the lifecycle of Kubernetes clusters. It simplifies the provisioning, upgrading, and operation of multiple clusters across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This is the official repository for Cluster API.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Cluster API to function.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.5/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains all necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API controller
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API controller using Helm, which manages the lifecycle of clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.5

```

> Ensure you have the correct namespace and version.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource to define a new cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureProviderCluster' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api

```

> Look for the cluster-api-controller pod to ensure it's running.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this will result in data loss for any clusters managed by Cluster API.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.5/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This removes the Cluster API controller.
> This removes the CRDs.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your cluster state. This step upgrades the Cluster API controller and verifies the installation.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o json > cluster-backup.json

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.5

kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api

```

> Backup the current cluster state.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the cluster-api-controller pod is running.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD has not been installed or is not available.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.5/cluster-api-components.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means that the Cluster API controller is not responding, possibly due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the resource allocation for the cluster-api-controller pod and ensure it has sufficient CPU and memory.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: infrastructure provider not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified infrastructure provider is not installed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the correct infrastructure provider CRDs and controllers are installed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <ip>:<port>: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Check the Kubernetes cluster status and ensure the API server is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved error handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API version for infrastructure providers.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your custom resources to the new API version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters' showing your cluster with a status of 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF
kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required tools installed.
